initial_program:
% ----- Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
nRows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
nCols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R)    :- R >= 0, R < N.
col(C)    :- C >= 0, C < M.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ----- Background colour (the colour at the topleft corner) ----------
bg(Col) :- input(0,0,Col).

% ----- Translation offsets for every nonbackground colour -------------
% dr(Col) = vertical offset, dc(Col) = horizontal offset.
% The two choice rules give exactly one value for each colour that
% is not background.
1 { dr(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) } 1 { dc(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }.

% ----- Apply the translation to all nonbackground cells ----------------
output(Rt, Ct, Col) :-
    input(R, C, Col),
    not bg(Col),
    Rt = R + dr(Col),
    Ct = C + dc(Col).

% ----- The translated positions must stay inside the grid ---------------
:- output(Rt, Ct, Col), Rt >= 0, Rt < N, Ct >= 0, Ct < M.

% ----- Every output cell gets exactly one colour -----------------------
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ----- Background cells stay unchanged (they are not moved) ----------
output(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), bg(Col).

% ----- No nonbackground colour may appear at a background cell -------
:- output(R, C, Col), not bg(Col), input(R, C, bg).

% ----- Ensure that the set of colours placed at output cells is exactly
% ----- the multiset of colours that appear in the input (nonbackground) 
% ----- This is enforced implicitly by the bijection created above.

#show output/3.

% ----- Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
nRows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
nCols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R)    :- R >= 0, R < N.
col(C)    :- C >= 0, C < M.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ----- Background colour (the colour at the topleft corner) ----------
bg(Col) :- input(0,0,Col).

% ----- Translation offsets for every nonbackground colour -------------
% dr(Col) = vertical offset, dc(Col) = horizontal offset.
% The two choice rules give exactly one value for each colour that
% is not background.
1 { dr(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }, 1 { dc(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }.

% ----- Apply the translation to all nonbackground cells ----------------
output(Rt, Ct, Col) :-
    input(R, C, Col),
    not bg(Col),
    Rt = R + dr(Col),
    Ct = C + dc(Col).

% ----- The translated positions must stay inside the grid ---------------
:- output(Rt, Ct, Col), Rt >= 0, Rt < N, Ct >= 0, Ct < M.

% ----- Every output cell gets exactly one colour -----------------------
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) }, 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ----- Background cells stay unchanged (they are not moved) ----------
output(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), bg(Col).

% ----- No nonbackground colour may appear at a background cell -------
:- output(R, C, Col), not bg(Col), input(R, C, bg).

% ----- Ensure that the set of colours placed at output cells is exactly
% ----- the multiset of colours that appear in the input (nonbackground) 
% ----- This is enforced implicitly by the bijection created above.

rounds.program_after:
% ----- Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
nRows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
nCols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R)    :- R >= 0, R < N.
col(C)    :- C >= 0, C < M.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ----- Background colour (the colour at the topleft corner) ----------
bg(Col) :- input(0,0,Col).

% ----- Translation offsets for every nonbackground colour -------------
% dr(Col) = vertical offset, dc(Col) = horizontal offset.
% The two choice rules give exactly one value for each colour that
% is not background.
1 { dr(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }, 1 { dc(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }.

% ----- Apply the translation to all nonbackground cells ----------------
output(Rt, Ct, Col) :-
    input(R, C, Col),
    not bg(Col),
    Rt = R + dr(Col),
    Ct = C + dc(Col).

% ----- The translated positions must stay inside the grid ---------------
:- output(Rt, Ct, Col), Rt >= 0, Rt < N, Ct >= 0, Ct < M.

% ----- Every output cell gets exactly one colour -----------------------
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) }, 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ----- Background cells stay unchanged (they are not moved) ----------
output(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), bg(Col).

% ----- No nonbackground colour may appear at a background cell -------
:- output(R, C, Col), not bg(Col), input(R, C, bg).

% ----- Ensure that the set of colours placed at output cells is exactly
% ----- the multiset of colours that appear in the input (nonbackground) 
% ----- This is enforced implicitly by the bijection created above.

#show output/3.

final_program:
% ----- Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
nRows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
nCols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R)    :- R >= 0, R < N.
col(C)    :- C >= 0, C < M.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ----- Background colour (the colour at the topleft corner) ----------
bg(Col) :- input(0,0,Col).

% ----- Translation offsets for every nonbackground colour -------------
% dr(Col) = vertical offset, dc(Col) = horizontal offset.
% The two choice rules give exactly one value for each colour that
% is not background.
1 { dr(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }, 1 { dc(Col) : color(Col), not bg(Col) }.

% ----- Apply the translation to all nonbackground cells ----------------
output(Rt, Ct, Col) :-
    input(R, C, Col),
    not bg(Col),
    Rt = R + dr(Col),
    Ct = C + dc(Col).

% ----- The translated positions must stay inside the grid ---------------
:- output(Rt, Ct, Col), Rt >= 0, Rt < N, Ct >= 0, Ct < M.

% ----- Every output cell gets exactly one colour -----------------------
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) }, 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ----- Background cells stay unchanged (they are not moved) ----------
output(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), bg(Col).

% ----- No nonbackground colour may appear at a background cell -------
:- output(R, C, Col), not bg(Col), input(R, C, bg).

% ----- Ensure that the set of colours placed at output cells is exactly
% ----- the multiset of colours that appear in the input (nonbackground) 
% ----- This is enforced implicitly by the bijection created above.

#show output/3.
